title: MailerLite vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare MailerLite and Drip email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which platform suits your business best.
h1: MailerLite vs Drip: Complete Comparison for Email Marketing
intro: MailerLite and Drip are both popular email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences and use cases. MailerLite targets budget-conscious small businesses with a free tier and affordable plans, while Drip focuses on e-commerce businesses seeking advanced tracking and revenue attribution. Understanding their differences will help you choose the right tool for your specific needs.

comparison_table.tool_a: Drag-drop editor, landing pages, pop-ups, surveys
comparison_table.tool_b: Revenue tracking, SMS, advanced segmentation, onsite campaigns

choose_a_if: You're a small business or freelancer on a tight budget
You want to start email marketing with zero upfront investment
You need landing pages, pop-ups, and surveys built into your platform
You prefer user-friendly tools that don't require technical expertise
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce store and need revenue attribution
Advanced automation and SMS marketing are priorities
You want onsite campaigns and deep store integration
You're willing to invest in a specialized e-commerce solution

faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your needs. MailerLite excels for small businesses and those on a budget, offering a free tier and user-friendly interface. Drip is superior for e-commerce businesses that need advanced tracking and revenue attribution. Choose based on your business type and budget.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, MailerLite or Drip?
faq.a: MailerLite is significantly cheaper, starting at free for up to 1,000 subscribers and $9/month for paid plans. Drip has no free tier and starts at $39/month. For budget-conscious businesses, MailerLite is the clear winner on cost.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to MailerLite?
faq.a: Yes, you can switch between platforms. Both tools support standard email export formats and integrations that make migration possible. However, you may lose some platform-specific features like Drip's revenue tracking when switching. Plan your migration carefully to minimize disruption.
